questions.question: Jahangir means '________' in Persian.
questions.explanation: Prince Salim took the title Jahangir, meaning 'Conqueror of the World' in Persian, after becoming emperor in 1605.

questions.question: Jahangir installed the ________ outside his palace to hear grievances directly.
questions.explanation: Soon after ascending the throne, Jahangir allowed a chain with bells outside his palace so that anyone could ask for justice. This was called the Chain of Justice.

questions.question: Prince Khusrau's rebellion took place in ________ in 1606.
questions.explanation: Prince Khusrau rebelled against his father Jahangir in Lahore in 1606, supported by powerful nobles.

questions.question: Guru Arjan Dev had given shelter to Prince Khusrau at ________.
questions.explanation: The fifth Sikh Guru Arjan Dev gave shelter to Prince Khusrau at Taran Taran and was subsequently executed by Jahangir.

questions.question: Rana Amar Singh of Mewar was the son of ________.
questions.explanation: Rana Amar Singh, the ruler of Mewar who submitted to the Mughals in 1615, was the son of the famous Rana Pratap.

questions.question: Kangra Fort surrendered to Jahangir on ________.
questions.explanation: The subjugation of Kangra Fort, which surrendered on November 16, 1620, was one of Jahangir's military achievements.

questions.question: ________ of Persia captured Kandahar from the Mughals in June 1622.
questions.explanation: Shah Abbas of Persia (1587-1629) captured Kandahar in June 1622, dealing a severe blow to Mughal power in Central Asia.

questions.question: ________ was sent by the East India Company to Jahangir's court in 1608.
questions.explanation: In 1608, the East India Company sent Captain William Hawkins to Jahangir's court to secure royal patronage for trading.

questions.question: The East India Company received its charter from Queen Elizabeth I on ________.
questions.explanation: A group of English merchants formed the East India Company in 1599 and received a royal charter from Queen Elizabeth I on December 31, 1600.

questions.question: In 1639, the British obtained a lease of ________ from a local king and built Fort St. George.
questions.explanation: In 1639, the East India Company obtained a lease of Madras from the local king and built Fort St. George, which became their headquarters.

questions.question: Nur Jahan was the only Mughal ________ who had coins minted with her name.
questions.explanation: Nur Jahan was the only Mughal empress (not emperor) who had coins minted with her name, an extraordinary distinction.

questions.question: Shah Jahan successfully ruled the Mughal Empire for ________ years.
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan successfully ruled the empire for 30 years, during which the Mughal Empire thrived and his reign came to be known as the golden era.

questions.question: The Taj Mahal was built in memory of Shah Jahan's wife ________.
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan is best known for the construction of the Taj Mahal in memory of his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.question: Shah Jahan's Peacock Throne contained the famous 186-carat ________ diamond.
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan commissioned the jewel-encrusted Peacock Throne which contained the famous 186-carat Koh-i-Noor diamond, later taken by the British.

questions.question: Aurangzeb assumed the title ________ meaning 'Conqueror of the World'.
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb took the title Aurangzeb Alamgir, where Alamgir means 'Conqueror of the World'. He ruled for fifty years.

questions.question: Aurangzeb defeated Dara's forces at ________ and Samugarh.
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb defeated Dara Shikoh's forces at Dharmat and Samugarh in 1657 and 1658 during the war of succession.

questions.question: The Jats revolted in the ________ region under the leadership of Gokula.
questions.explanation: In 1669-1670, the Jats rose in the Mathura region under the leadership of Gokula against Aurangzeb's policies.

questions.question: Guru Tegh Bahadur was executed in December 1675 in ________.
questions.explanation: Guru Tegh Bahadur, the ninth Sikh Guru, was captured, taken to Delhi, asked to embrace Islam, refused, and was executed in December 1675 in Chandni Chowk.

questions.question: Aurangzeb spent the last ________ years of his reign in the Deccan.
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb spent the last twenty-six years of his reign in the Deccan, annexing Bijapur and Golconda and fighting the Marathas.

questions.question: Shivaji signed the Treaty of Purandhar in ________.
questions.explanation: Shivaji was forced to sign the Treaty of Purandhar in 1665 after Raja Jai Singh besieged the fort of Purandhar. He had to surrender 23 of his 35 forts.
questions.source_section: Rise of the Marathas and Chhatrapati Shivaji

questions.question: Shivaji managed to escape from the Mughal court and reached ________ in 1666.
questions.explanation: Shivaji was ill-treated and taken prisoner at the Mughal court in Agra but managed to escape and reach Raigarh in 1666.
questions.source_section: Rise of the Marathas and Chhatrapati Shivaji

questions.question: Shah Jahan was called the '________' and the 'Engineer King' for his love of architecture.
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan was called the 'Prince among Builders' and the 'Engineer King' for his exceptional love of architecture.

questions.question: The construction of the Taj Mahal began in 1632 and was completed by ________.
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal, Shah Jahan's grandest project, had its construction begin in 1632 and was completed by 1643.

questions.question: ________ by Khafi Khan covers the history of the Mughal period.
questions.explanation: Muntakhab-ul-Lubab by Khafi Khan covers the history of the Mughal period and was written during Aurangzeb's reign.

questions.question: The city of ________, now known as Old Delhi, was built by Shah Jahan.
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the city of Shahjahanabad, which is now known as Old Delhi, as one of his many architectural achievements.
